Public debt rises to $286.8 bn; debt-to-GDP ratio hits 70%</title><description><![CDATA[Pakistan's public debt surged to $286.832 billion by June 2025, a nearly 13% increase from the previous year, pushing the debt-to-GDP ratio to 70%. This rise is attributed to lower-than-expected GDP growth and subdued inflation. Despite these challenges, the economy shows signs of recovery with IMF support and improved external reserves.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/international-business/pakistans-economy-collapsing-public-debt-rises-to-286-8-billion-debt-to-gdp-ratio-hits-70/articleshow/124809439.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/international-business/pakistans-economy-collapsing-public-debt-rises-to-286-8-billion-debt-to-gdp-ratio-hits-70/articleshow/124809439.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 25 Oct 2025 21:43:21 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI BUSINESS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="687538" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124809580,imgsize-687538.cms"/></item><item><title>'You can call me Raja': Indian-origin Congressman shuts down 'racist' Florida leader</title><description><![CDATA[Chandler Langevin is a member of the Palm Bay City Council who made headlines because of his hateful statements against Indians. The veteran batter, a consistent performer Down Under, leaves a remarkable legacy with numerous centuries and impressive averages across formats, etching unforgettable moments at the Sydney Cricket Ground.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/cricket/india-vs-australia/virat-kohli-walks-away-happy-ending-to-a-beautiful-story/articleshow/124801664.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/cricket/india-vs-australia/virat-kohli-walks-away-happy-ending-to-a-beautiful-story/articleshow/124801664.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 25 Oct 2025 16:13:47 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI SPORTS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="96002" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124801862,imgsize-96002.cms"/></item><item><title>Bihar polls: Tejashwi vows state development; calls Nitish 'incapable of leading'</title><description><![CDATA[Tejashwi Yadav promises to transform Bihar into a leading state by attracting investments and establishing factories. Yadav criticizes the NDA government and vows to bring development to Bihar if the INDIA bloc wins power.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/we-need-to-make-bihar-number-one-tejashwi-yadav-vows-development-investments-calls-nitish-kumar-incapable-of-leading-anymore/articleshow/124804874.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/we-need-to-make-bihar-number-one-tejashwi-yadav-vows-development-investments-calls-nitish-kumar-incapable-of-leading-anymore/articleshow/124804874.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 25 Oct 2025 16:48:04 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI NEWS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="787302" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124804993,imgsize-787302.cms"/></item><item><title>'Sau chuhe kha kar, billi haj ko chali': Shah mocks Tejashwi over crime remarks</title><description><![CDATA[]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/sau-chuhe-kha-kar-billi-haj-ko-chali-amit-shah-mocks-tejashwi-over-crime-remarks-hits-out-at-rahul-gandhis-ghuspetiya-bachao-yatra/articleshow/124803285.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/sau-chuhe-kha-kar-billi-haj-ko-chali-amit-shah-mocks-tejashwi-over-crime-remarks-hits-out-at-rahul-gandhis-ghuspetiya-bachao-yatra/articleshow/124803285.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 25 Oct 2025 15:15:03 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI NEWS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="1076781" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124803404,imgsize-1076781.cms"/></item><item><title>IND vs AUS: Only behind Tendulkar! Kohli rewrites record books at SCG</title><description><![CDATA[Virat Kohli achieved another significant milestone, becoming the second-highest run-scorer in ODI history, surpassing Kumar Sangakkara. He also equaled Sachin Tendulkar's record for most 50+ scores against Australia in ODIs. Kohli's composed half-century anchored India's chase, showcasing his prowess as a "Chasemaster".]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/cricket/india-vs-australia/ind-vs-aus-only-behind-great-sachin-tendulkar-virat-kohli-rewrites-record-books-at-scg/articleshow/124803405.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/cricket/india-vs-australia/ind-vs-aus-only-behind-great-sachin-tendulkar-virat-kohli-rewrites-record-books-at-scg/articleshow/124803405.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 25 Oct 2025 15:25:17 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI SPORTS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="263700" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124803594,imgsize-263700.cms"/></item><item><title>Satish Shah passes away at 74: Fans pay tribute to 'Dilwale Dulhaniya Le Jayenge' actor</title><description><![CDATA[Veteran actor Satish Shah, celebrated for his comedic roles in 'Sarabhai Vs Sarabhai' and 'Jaane Bhi Do Yaaro', has passed away at 74. The actor, who had been battling kidney issues, left a lasting legacy in Indian cinema and television. His demise has prompted widespread tributes from fans remembering his memorable performances and the laughter he brought to audiences.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/entertainment/hindi/bollywood/news/satish-shah-passes-away-at-74-fans-pay-tribute-to-dilwale-dulhaniya-le-jayenge-actor/articleshow/124805579.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/entertainment/hindi/bollywood/news/satish-shah-passes-away-at-74-fans-pay-tribute-to-dilwale-dulhaniya-le-jayenge-actor/articleshow/124805579.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 25 Oct 2025 17:28:00 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI ENTERTAINMENT DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="28824" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124805579,imgsize-28824.cms"/></item></channel></rss>

      <description><![CDATA[India's trade delegation has concluded its visit to the US, with government sources reporting that discussions with American officials progressed smoothly and "no major differences" emerged.     <item>
      <title><![CDATA[Polio-Like Moment in Vision Care? Lenskart’s Plan To Be The Eyewear Capital Of The World ]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/access/watchlist/business/polio-like-moment-in-vision-care-lenskarts-plan-to-be-the-eyewear-capital-of-the-world-19720140.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[From disrupting a dull category to transforming eyewear into a lifestyle, Lenskart has reimagined how India sees.With 2,700+ stores across 14 countries and 22 million units sold in FY25, it now ranks among Asia’s top two spectacles retailers by volume and is now eyeing an IPO estimated at ₹7,500–₹8,000 crore.On the latest episode of Young Turks Reloaded, Shereen Bhan catches up with Peyush Bansal, Chairman, MD & CEO of Lenskart, on the vision, breakthroughs, and bold bets driving the brand’s global ambition.When Lenskart began, organised eyewear retail in India was minimal, and glasses were seen mainly as medical prescriptions. “Half of India’s population needs glasses, yet two-thirds don’t wear any,” Bansal notes. Lenskart transformed the consumer experience, making eyewear fun and accessible through virtual try-ons, personalised experiences, and removing the “patient” label.  While India is often called the “blind capital of the world,” Lenskart aspires to become the “eyewear capital of the world.” But the reality is stark: India has only 60 eyewear stores per million people, compared to 1,200 jewellery stores. From the first pitch to filing the DRHP, the mission has remained constant: provide vision to a billion people. The Lenskart CEO envisions a future where eye tests can be conducted on smartphones, dramatically increasing accessibility and potentially eliminating myopia-related challenges - what Bansal calls the “polio moment” for vision care, because he believes, “Everyone should have the right to see.”]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Fri, 17 Oct 2025 16:06:38 +0530]]></pubDate>
      <dc:creator>Zenia Baria</dc:creator>
      <guid><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/access/watchlist/business/polio-like-moment-in-vision-care-lenskarts-plan-to-be-the-eyewear-capital-of-the-world-19720140.htm]]></guid>
      <category><![CDATA[Business]]></category>
      <media:content height="675" width="1200" medium="image" url="https://images.cnbctv18.com/uploads/2025/10/1760697420_IwFBtjWI10-YT-RELOADED-EP-THUMBSpng-MKZqfa0k58.png"/>
    </item>

      <title><![CDATA[Bajaj Auto expects key approval for KTM takeover in November, Rajiv Bajaj says restructuring underway]]></title>

      <description><![CDATA[Rajiv Bajaj said KTM’s European operations could significantly improve efficiency by cutting overhead costs by more than half across areas such as R&D, marketing, and administration. He noted that the company has already reduced its workforce from 6,000 to 4,000 employees, with most being white-collar staff, and pointed out that excessive management layers have added to the organisation’s inefficiency.]]></description>
